rulings.Calendar Matter: Joint Notice Of Merit Hearing On Petitioner'S Petition For Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for March 19, 2026, line 1. RESPONDENT STEVEN GORDON'S Joint Notice Of Merit Hearing On Petitioner's Petition For Writ Of Mandate. Off calendar without prejudice to being re-noticed for a hearing at least ten court days after delivery of courtesy copies of all papers filed in support and in opposition to the petition and a stipulation identifying the administrative record or, if there is no administrative record, identifying the documents and other materials which the court is requested to consider in ruling on the petition. Neither side complied with the local rule requiring delivery of courtesy copies to department 301 and must do so at least ten court days in advance of the new hearing date. IF THIS TENTATIVE IS CONTESTED THE MATTER WILL BE HEARD BY JUDGE KAHN ON MARCH 24, 2026 at 10:00 AM For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROSA MARTINEZ VS. AIRBNB, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendants? First Set Of Requests For Admission To Plaintiff; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for March 19, 2026, line 2. DEFENDANTS AIRBNB, INC., AIRBNB PAYMENTS, LLC'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendants? First Set Of Requests For Admission To Plaintiff. Defendants' motion to compel further responses to requests 9-12 in their first set of requests for admissions is denied. Plaintiff's responses comply with the intent and spirit of CCP 2033.220 and are not evasive. Although plaintiff does not use the statutorily required phrase that "a reasonable inquiry concerning the matter in the .. request has been made," her amended responses show that she believes that she has made a reasonable inquiry and nothing substantive would be achieved by requiring her to amend her responses to include the quoted language. That defendants clearly (and likely in good faith) believe that their requests should be admitted does not warrant compelling plaintiff to provide further responses. Defendants' remedy is to seek cost of proof sanctions in the event that they are able to prove the truth of their requests. IF THIS TENTATIVE IS CONTESTED THE MATTER WILL BE HEARD BY JUDGE KAHN ON MARCH 24, 2026 at 10:00 AM For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROSA MARTINEZ VS. AIRBNB, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Response To Request For Production (Set Two) Number 43 From Airbnb Defendants
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for March 19, 2026, line 2. PLAINTIFF ROSA MARTINEZ'S Motion To Compel Further Response To Request For Production (Set Two) Number 43 From Airbnb Defendants. Plaintiff's motion to compel further response to request 43 in her second set of document requests is granted. Defendants must produce all responsive documents no later than April 17, 2026 and serve a verified response that they have done so. The request seeks discovery relevant information and none of defendants' objections are supported by any evidence or authority. IF THIS TENTATIVE IS CONTESTED THE MATTER WILL BE HEARD BY JUDGE KAHN ON MARCH 24, 2026 at 10:00 AM For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. rulings.Case Title: MAYA BLYTH VS. LEAL UGRIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion For A Protective Order To Control The Sequence And Timing Of Depositions And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for March 19, 2026, line 3. PLAINTIFF MAYA BLYTH'S Motion For A Protective Order To Control The Sequence And Timing Of Depositions And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Plaintiff Maya Blyth's motion for a protective order to control the sequence of depositions and for monetary sanctions is denied. Blyth has not shown good cause for the protective order she seeks, although the motion was not opposed. Neither side is entitled to a specific sequencing of depositions. IF THIS TENTATIVE IS CONTESTED THE MATTER WILL BE HEARD BY JUDGE KAHN ON MARCH 24, 2026 at 10:00 AM For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. rulings.Case Title: MAYA BLYTH VS. LEAL UGRIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Leal Ugrin To Resume The Court Ordered In Person Deposition, Produce Documents Pursuant To Court Order And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for March 19, 2026, line 3. PLAINTIFF MAYA BLYTH'S Motion To Compel Defendant Leal Ugrin To Resume The Court Ordered In Person Deposition, Produce Documents Pursuant To Court Order And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Plaintiff Maya Blyth's motion to compel defendant Leal Ugrin to resume his deposition and produce documents and for monetary sanctions is granted. Ugrin must make himself available in-person for a further session of his deposition on a mutually convenient date, produce all requested documents and he and his attorneys pay reasonable monetary sanctions of $2,000 no later than April 17, 2026. Good cause shown and no opposition filed. IF THIS TENTATIVE IS CONTESTED THE MATTER WILL BE HEARD BY JUDGE KAHN ON MARCH 24, 2026 at 10:00 AM For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RANDALL MCCUIN VS. SIAVASH DEHGHANI ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for March 19, 2026, line 6. DEFENDANTS AZAR SAEIDI, SOOTHING DENTAL'S DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT. Defendants' (Azar Sona Saeidi and Soothing Dental, Inc.) demurrer to the first amended complaint (FAC) is sustained without leave to amend as to the first cause of action, sustained with leave to amend as to the second cause of action, and overruled as to the second cause of action. The third cause of action is stricken because it was added outside the scope of the prior leave to amend. The court's tentative ruling in its entirety has been sent to counsel via email. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)
